content_html:
MKT10009 Marketing and the Consumer ExperienceLecture 11
Chapter 14 from our Textbook: Principles of Marketing,
8th edition
Presented by Dr Gordon Campbell

Learning Objectives for Lecture 11 Part 2
14.2 Identify the main social criticisms of marketing.

Part 2 – Social criticisms of marketing

Social criticisms of marketing (1 of 4)
Marketing’s impact on individual customers
Social critics claim that certain marketing practices hurt individual consumers, society as a whole and other business firms, e.g.
high prices
deceptive practices
high-pressure selling
shoddy or unsafe products
planned obsolescence
poor service to disadvantaged consumers.
Q: Such actions harm a company’s business in the long run so why would marketers practise them?

Social criticisms of marketing (2 of 4)
Marketing’s impact on society as a whole
The marketing system has been accused of adding to several ‘evils’ to society at large.
These practices include:
false wants and too much materialism
too few social goods
cultural pollution.
Q: Marketers might justify these and other similar practices on the basis that consumers have alternatives. Do you agree? Why, or why not?

Social criticisms of marketing (3 of 4)
Marketing’s impact on other businesses
Critics charge that a company’s marketing practices can harm other companies and reduce competition.
These practices include:
acquisitions of competitors
marketing practices that create barriers to entry
unfair competitive marketing practices
predatory pricing.
Q: At what point do you think a ‘competitive marketing practice’ becomes a ‘marketing abuse’?

Social criticisms of marketing (4 of 4)
Q: Legislation and government agencies can be used to moderate competitive marketing practices. Do you think this is necessary or appropriate?
Consumerism: The ACCC provides information about unit pricing to help consumers make more informed decisions. In this example, the higher-priced laundry detergent has the lower unit price.

Learning Objective 2 – check that you can >>>
14.2 Identify the main social criticisms of marketing.
